title: Infinity Mod Forge — Author Metadata
description: Metadata file that mod authors include in their repository to help Infinity Mod Forge stay up to date. Place this file as mod-forge.json in the root of your mod repository. For the evidence-grading model on conflicts/advisories, see docs/EVIDENCE_GRADING.md in the Forge repo.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor autocompletion.

homepage.description: URL to the mod's homepage, forum thread, or GitHub releases page.

summary.description: One-sentence description of what the mod does (max ~200 chars).

games.description: Which game phases this mod affects.

tags.description: Content tags for filtering. Use established tags when possible.

languages.description: Map of ISO 639-1 language code to WeiDU LANGUAGE index. Example: {"en": 0, "fr": 1, "de": 2}

components.description: List of installable components (matches BEGIN blocks in the tp2).

conflicts.description: Observed or mechanism-verified conflicts with other mods. Items must carry an `evidenceLevel`. Soft/untested interactions belong in `advisories[]` instead.

advisories.description: Potential interactions with other mods — plausible mechanisms, order-only concerns, or speculative compatibility notes that have not been verified against an actual install failure. Rendered separately from `conflicts[]` in the Forge UI (neutral tone, not a red flag).

dependencies.description: Mods that must be installed before this one.

knownIssues.description: Known installation errors or warnings with workarounds.

name.description: Component display name (as shown by WeiDU during install).

number.description: Component number (DESIGNATED value, or auto-numbered position).

group.description: SUBCOMPONENT group name. Components sharing the same group are mutually exclusive choices.

label.description: WeiDU LABEL for this component (if defined in tp2).

deprecated.description: Set true if this component is no longer recommended.

removed.description: Set true if this component was removed from the tp2 but should be tracked for compatibility.

notes.description: Any installation caveats, prerequisites, or special instructions for this component.

kits.description: Number of ADD_KIT calls this component makes (for engine kit-cap tracking).

spells.description: Number of new SPELL.IDS entries this component adds.

splstates.description: Number of new SPLSTATE.IDS entries this component adds.

items.description: Number of new items this component adds.

mod.description: TP2 folder name of the conflicting mod.

severity.description: hard = mutually exclusive, partial = specific components conflict, soft = possible issues.

evidenceLevel.description: How strongly this conflict is supported by evidence. `observed` = an actual install run has produced the failure (session ID or WSETUP.DEBUG excerpt must be cited in `sessionId` or `source`). `mechanism-verified` = code paths in both mods have been traced and the failure mode is mechanically plausible, but has not yet been seen in practice. `speculative` = plausible but unverified — these belong in `advisories[]`, not `conflicts[]`. Forge UI renders the three tiers with different severity styling.

sessionId.description: Required when `evidenceLevel = "observed"`. Session ID from the runner's install log (8+ hex characters), e.g. "deeae982" or a `Test #N` tag. Lets reviewers verify the claim against preserved WSETUP.DEBUG.

source.description: Free-form provenance note — upstream mod's README/readme advisory, forum thread URL, author-reported incompatibility, code-trace rationale, etc. Distinct from `sessionId` (which is an install-run reference).

orderOnly.description: True when the conflict only manifests under a specific install order, and installing in the recommended order resolves it. The Forge UI can suppress order-only conflicts from the top-severity view since they don't require user intervention.

reason.description: Why these mods conflict. Prefer concrete mechanism (`Mod A does COPY_EXISTING X.SPL which Mod B's cleanup removes from override`) over vague phrasing (`may interact with`).

myComponents.description: Which of YOUR component numbers are involved (e.g. "#0 #10").

theirComponents.description: Which of THEIR component numbers are involved.

then.errorMessage: evidenceLevel=observed requires either sessionId or source to cite the install run.

mod.description: TP2 folder name of the required mod.

type.description: hard = required for functionality, soft = recommended.

pattern.description: Regex pattern that matches this issue in WeiDU debug logs.

workaround.description: How to fix or avoid it.

components.description: Which component numbers are affected (empty = all).

forum.description: Link to a forum thread discussing this issue.
